code: lfs_pumf <- get_pumf("LFS", refresh="auto") unemployment_stats <- lfs_pumf |>    filter(LFSSTAT !="Not in labour force") |>   filter(AGE_12 %in% c("25 to 29 years","30 to 34 years", "35 to 39 years")) |>   mutate(jd=case_when(is.na(DURJLESS) ~ "Not applicable",                       DURJLESS<12 ~ "Less than one year",                       TRUE ~ "One year or more")) |>   add_lfs_SURVDATE() |>   summarize(Count=sum(FINALWT),.by=c(SURVDATE,jd,AGE_12)) |>   mutate(Share=Count/sum(Count),.by=c(SURVDATE,AGE_12)) |>   filter(jd!="Not applicable")   unemployment_stats |>   ggplot(aes(x=SURVDATE,y=Share,colour=AGE_12)) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~jd) +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent_format()) +   labs(title="Unemployment by duration of unemployment",        y="Unemployment rate",x=NULL,        colour="Age group",        caption="StatCan LFS (PUMF)") #> Warning: Missing values are always removed in SQL aggregation functions. #> Use `na.rm = TRUE` to silence this warning #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. microbenchmark::microbenchmark(collect(unemployment_stats)) |>    boxplot() #> Warning in microbenchmark::microbenchmark(collect(unemployment_stats)): less #> accurate nanosecond times to avoid potential integer overflows lfs_pumf |>    filter(LFSSTAT !="Not in labour force") |>   add_lfs_SURVDATE() |>   add_lfs_GENDER_SEX() |>   summarise(Count=sum(FINALWT),.by=c(SURVDATE,LFSSTAT,GENDER_SEX)) |>   mutate(Share=Count/sum(Count),.by=c(SURVDATE,GENDER_SEX)) |>   filter(LFSSTAT=="Unemployed") |>   ggplot(aes(x=SURVDATE,y=Share,colour=GENDER_SEX)) +   geom_line() +   scale_y_continuous(labels=scales::percent_format()) +   labs(title="Unemployment sex/gender",        y="Unemployment rate",x=NULL,        colour="Gender",        caption="StatCan LFS (PUMF)") lfs_pumf |> close_pumf()

what: Where the weights are stored (DuckDB path)
title: Bootstrap weights
text: replicate weights copied main survey table. Instead: written separate DuckDB table, auto-named paste0("pumf_bsw_", tolower(weight_col)) (e.g. pumf_bsw_pweight). auto-naming means generating weights two different weight columns (e.g. household weight person weight) produces two independent BSW tables never collide. companion VIEW (e.g. eng_bsw_pweight) joins survey table BSW table row identifier, returned tbl exposes every survey column plus replicate columns. bsw_info() summarises stored: separation keeps main table untouched lets add, inspect, remove replicate weights independently survey data.
code: bsw_info(cis_bsw) #> # A tibble: 1 × 6 #>   weight_col bsw_table        view_name       view_exists n_replicates size_mb #>   <chr>      <chr>            <chr>           <lgl>              <int>   <dbl> #> 1 FWEIGHT    pumf_bsw_fweight eng_bsw_fweight TRUE                 200    0.07

title: Bootstrap weights
text: Many survey designs stratified, resampling respect : replicate resample within stratum, preserving stratum sample sizes. Pass strata_cols: Resolution order strata: strata_cols pass , otherwise registry bsw_strata survey, otherwise, LFS, default c("SURVYEAR", "SURVMNTH") month resampled separately, otherwise unstratified. Pass strata_cols = character(0) force unstratified weights even default exists (e.g. LFS).
code: cis_bsw <- cis |>   add_bootstrap_weights(weight_col = "FWEIGHT", strata_cols = "PROV",                         n_replicates = 200, seed = 42)

title: Bootstrap weights
text: get bootstrap standard error estimate, compute estimate original weights replicate weight; spread replicate estimates sampling variability. example estimates total population represented survey (sum weights), depends weight columns, pattern applies weighted statistic — replace sum(.x) estimator (weighted mean, share, total, …) evaluated weight column. pattern works group_by() / summarise(): carry CPBSW* columns summary, collapse standard error per group.
code: est <- cis_bsw |>   summarise(across(c(FWEIGHT, matches("^CPBSW[0-9]+$")), ~ sum(.x))) |>   collect() #> Warning: Missing values are always removed in SQL aggregation functions. #> Use `na.rm = TRUE` to silence this warning #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.  point_estimate      <- est$FWEIGHT replicate_estimates <- est |> select(matches("^CPBSW[0-9]+$")) |> unlist()  # Bootstrap variance: mean squared deviation of the replicate estimates from # the full-sample estimate; the standard error is its square root. # Confidence intervals can be obtained by taking the appropriate quantiles. confidence_interval <- quantile(replicate_estimates,c(0.025,0.975)) std_error <- sqrt(mean((replicate_estimates - point_estimate)^2))  c(estimate = point_estimate, se = std_error, conf=confidence_interval) #>   estimate         se  conf.2.5% conf.97.5%  #>   36831173     170660   36493061   37126328

what: Rows added to the survey table
title: Bootstrap weights
text: subtle case. bootstrap replicate resamples full population (full stratum), adding rows invalidates replicate weights affected resampling universe — simply generate weights new rows isolation. add_bootstrap_weights() detects rows weights deletes regenerates affected weights: Unstratified: whole population changed, every row’s replicate weights regenerated. Stratified: strata gained rows regenerated full; strata already complete keep existing weights. makes re-weighting updated multi-year file (example, appending new LFS month year) inexpensive — new strata recomputed. Extending number replicates adding rows can happen call; handled rows applies . Extending weighted table appending rows relies new rows valid identifier. Use natural id_col (registry bsw_join_key) expect grow table, new rows can matched.

what: Naming conventions and where to put the files
title: Onboarding a new PUMF
text: Everything lives single cache directory. Set , ideally .Rprofile, data persists across sessions: Without option cache falls back tempdir() discarded end session. cache organised strictly series version: two names choose — <series> <version> — identifiers pass get_pumf(series, version). conventions matter: series survey acronym used throughout package: "SHS", "SFS", "CHS", "GSS", "", "SGVP", "Census", "LFS", … Use existing acronym new year existing survey joins siblings. genuinely new survey pick short, stable acronym. version normally four-digit reference year ("2023"). Sticking bare year unlocks smart fallback described . Census uses multi-part versions ("2021 (individuals)") LFS accumulates versions one shared database — special-cased documented vignettes. deposit PUMF manually, create version directory drop StatCan zip (extracted files fine — canpumf unzip nested archives first use): surveys canpumf knows download, can skip manual copy entirely let get_pumf() fetch zip (see list_canpumf_collection() / list_pumf_registry()). Manual deposit case file brand-new, EFT-, otherwise yet wired collection table.
code: options(canpumf.cache_path = "~/data/pumf.data") <cache_path>/   <series>/     <version>/       <original>.zip              # the StatCan download, retained       <series>_<version>.duckdb   # built by canpumf       metadata/                   # canonical CSVs, written by canpumf         variables.csv         codes.csv         layout.csv                # only for fixed-width data dir.create("~/data/pumf.data/CHS/2025", recursive = TRUE) file.copy("~/Downloads/2025.zip", "~/data/pumf.data/CHS/2025/")

what: Smart defaults and the newest-sibling fallback
title: Onboarding a new PUMF
text: survey’s configuration lives registry (R/registry.R): file directory data file (file_mask), bootstrap-weight file joins (bsw_*), encodings, per-variable fixups. Surveys registry entry fall back auto-detection, inspects directory picks data file metadata parser heuristically. Two design choices make new years onboard little work: Generic year masks. Recent entries use \d{4} placeholder instead hard-coded year, e.g. CHS data file mask chs\d{4}ecl_pumf\.csv rather chs2022ecl_pumf\.csv. version directory contains exactly one year’s files, generic pattern resolves unambiguously new year needs edit mask. Newest-sibling config inheritance. request bare-year version registry entry, series least one year-keyed entry, pumf_registry_lookup() automatically reuses config newest sibling whose year one asked (oldest sibling year predates ). prints one-time message reuse visible: 2025 release follows naming layout 2022 — common case — just works: generic mask finds chs2025ecl_pumf.csv, BSW join wired way, metadata parsed command files shipped inside 2025 download (metadata always per-version; never borrowed sibling, variable positions codes drift cycles). Inheritance deliberately silent substitute explicit entry. Treat message prompt confirm new release really matches, add proper CHS/2025 entry . Inheritance skipped multi-part Census versions LFS. first thing try new bare-year release simply: columns look right labels resolve, done — go section 4 make permanent.
code: # Suppose CHS/2025 has just been released and is not in the registry yet. tbl <- get_pumf("CHS", "2025") #> No CHS/2025 registry entry; inheriting config from CHS/2022. Verify the new #> release matches (file layout, codes, BSW join) and add an explicit entry if #> it differs. tbl <- get_pumf("CHS", "2025") tbl |> label_pumf_columns() |> head() |> collect()

title: Onboarding a new PUMF
text: pumf_metadata() runs locate + parse stages returns canonical metadata (variables, codes, layout) without building DuckDB table. fast feedback loop: Good signs: every data column appears variables; categorical variables rows codes; fixed-width data, layout sensible start/end positions. Common problems registry field fixes : Wrong file chosen / “data file found” → set file_mask. Several command files, parser picks wrong one → set layout_mask disambiguate SPSS/SAS files. Garbled accents French labels / read errors → set metadata_encoding (default "CP1252"; older DOS-era files sometimes need "CP850", recent ones "UTF-8"). Labels missing entirely (DATA LIST-SPSS) → supply *Dictionary.pdf directory PDF parser can fill .
code: meta <- pumf_metadata("NEWSURVEY", "2025") str(meta$variables)   # one row per variable: name, label_en, label_fr, type, ... str(meta$codes)       # one row per code value: name, val, label_en, label_fr str(meta$layout)      # fixed-width column ranges (absent for CSV data)

what: Start from an existing entry as a template
title: Onboarding a new PUMF
text: rarely start blank slate. Inspect related entry — earlier year survey, different survey file format — use starting point: Build candidate configuration pumf_registry_entry(). fields supply recorded; rest fall back pipeline defaults. pass pumf_metadata() / get_pumf() via registry = argument without touching R/registry.R — perfect iterating: Note refresh = TRUE: parsing idempotent, metadata/ exists new registry effect force re-parse. adapting survey whose format matches existing one, literally copy survey’s entry fields pumf_registry_entry() adjust masks. exactly new CHS year reuses CHS/2022 shape, new survey might start configuration existing one shares file format.
code: pumf_registry("CHS", "2022")   # inspect a known entry list_pumf_registry()           # see everything that is registered entry <- pumf_registry_entry(   file_mask     = "PUMF_NEWSURVEY_\\d{4}\\.txt",  # generic year from the start   bsw_file_mask = "bsw_flatfile\\.txt",   bsw_join_key  = "CASEID" )  # Re-parse with the candidate config until the metadata looks right: meta <- pumf_metadata("NEWSURVEY", "2025", registry = entry, refresh = TRUE)

title: Onboarding a new PUMF
text: structure parses, build stage may still need per-variable adjustments. go data_fixups (see ?pumf_registry_entry full list): force_numeric — continuous variable carrying top-code/boundary labels; drops spurious codes first converts true-missing sentinels NA range. force_character / force_integer / force_bigint — override DuckDB storage type geographic codes keep leading zeros, --range IDs truncated. na_values — raw string values become NA across columns (undeclared sentinels, SAS-style "."). codes_supplement / missing_supplement — inject code rows missing ranges command files omit.
code: entry <- pumf_registry_entry(   file_mask   = "PUMF_NEWSURVEY_\\d{4}\\.txt",   data_fixups = list(     force_numeric  = "INCOME",     force_character = "GEOCODE"   ) )

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: pumf_locate_or_download() ensures version directory exists extracted content parsing begins. Cache layout: Decision sequence: refresh = TRUE — delete .duckdb file(s) metadata/ subdirectory, leaving zip extracted content untouched. Stages 2 3 re-run without re-downloading. redownload = TRUE — wipe entire version directory first, proceed first-time run. Implies refresh. Already extracted? — version_is_extracted() returns TRUE subdirectory (metadata/) non-zip non-duckdb file present. TRUE, zip step skipped even zip still disk. Download — URL looked list_canpumf_collection(). Surveys distributed via Statistics Canada’s EFT portal marker "(EFT)" instead URL; function stops instructions deposit zip manually. Extract — robust_unzip() handles two edge cases: Naming collision: zips contain single top-level directory name archive (e.g. 2025-CSV.zip/). colliding directory renamed strip .zip moved version directory. Encoding: older StatCan zips store filenames without UTF-8 flag (General Purpose Bit Flag bit 11). grep/sub calls zip entry names use useBytes = TRUE avoid “invalid locale” warnings.
code: <cache_path>/   <series>/     <version>/       <original>.zip          # retained after extraction       <extracted dirs>/       metadata/               # written by Stage 2       <series>_<version>.duckdb

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: detect_formats() scans entire version directory recursively identifies parser(s) apply. Multiple parsers can fire survey (e.g. SPSS split layout/codes SAS cards BSW weights). Detection case 5 also searches parallel French file — candidate set whose path includes /fran /french (case-insensitive).

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Handles single-file SPSS format used Census (2001–2021), SFS 1999, SHS, others. file typically contains DATA LIST, VARIABLE LABELS, VALUE LABELS, sometimes MISSING VALUES FORMATS sections. VARIABLE LABELS optional (e.g. Census 2011 individuals omits ). Older releases like SFS 1999 DATA LIST label sections — produce fully importable table raw codes human-readable factor levels. Key parsing details: Column ranges — DATA LIST ranges may spaces either side dash (129-135, 129 - 135, 129-  135). three normalised regex (\\d+)\\s*-\\s*(\\d+) tokenisation. Record-group marker — leading / first variable line (e.g. /PROVP  1-2) stripped, discarded, variable retained. Section terminator — DATA LIST section ends first blank line, . line, occurrence VARIABLE LABELS, VALUE LABELS, MISSING VALUES, FORMATS, EXECUTE start line. keyword check reliable terminator older files (e.g. 1991 XMF) blank line DATA LIST VARIABLE LABELS. DATA LIST type annotations — () suffix column range marks character-type variable. parser records is_char flag per column uses populate variables.csv types VARIABLE LABELS section present. Sentinel detection — variables whose VALUE LABELS sentinel phrases (“applicable”, “Valid skip”, “Don’t know”, “Data available”, etc.) classified numeric missing_low/missing_high range, character. prevents spurious NA warnings numeric values fall outside label set. Zero-padded codes — unquoted SPSS numeric codes like 01, 02 normalised via .numeric() → .character() match bare integer values CSV data. Multi-variable VALUE LABELS blocks — /VAR1 VAR2 VAR3 headers (possibly spanning continuation lines) fully parsed listed variables receive code/label pairs.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Used SFS, CPSS, similar surveys ship separate files variable labels (*vare.sps), value labels (*vale.sps), missing values (*miss.sps), layout (*_i.sps). layout_mask registry disambiguates single directory holds multiple sets (e.g. individual vs. household files).

Extension pre-filter — derived registry file_mask: Several subdirectories always excluded search: metadata/, SPSS/, Command/, Syntax/, Layout/, SpssCard/, Reading_cards/, Documents/. text: data file identified: FWF metadata/layout.csv exists data file end .csv. handles edge case (e.g. CHS) SPSS DATA LIST produces layout actual data ships CSV. CSV otherwise. paths read columns character (col_types = cols(.default = "c")) preserve leading zeros avoid premature type coercion. Numeric conversion happens explicitly next step.

text: reading fixed-width file, row fewer two columns non-NA dropped. FWF files older StatCan archives often end \r\n\x1a (DOS EOF marker), FWF reader interprets one-character row single non-NA field; step removes silently. CSV files affected — CSV parsers handle trailing newlines correctly.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: Registry data_fixups entries applied raw character data label mapping: str_pad — left- right-pad specified columns target width. Used zero-pad codes arrive without leading zeros CSV formats (e.g. SFS). rename — rename column; applied old name present (safe surveys ship multiple release variants, e.g. Census 2021 RELIG/RELIGION_DER). cols_swap — named character vector c(= "B", C = "D") swapping pairs column names. Used surveys DATA LIST variable names transposed relative PDF documentation (e.g. WKACTMA/WKACTFA FAOCC81/MAOCC81 Census 1981 individuals). force_numeric — character vector column names treat numeric regardless many VALUE LABELS declared. Used variable carries boundary top-code labels (e.g. "85 years ") alongside otherwise-continuous values, integer index SPSS file mis-classifies categorical (e.g. SUBSAMPL Census 1971). codes dropped, true-missing sentinel codes (stated, Don’t know, Valid skip, … — zero-value labels like “None”) first converted per-variable missing_low/missing_high range sentinels still become NA. existing missing range (MISSING VALUES split-SPSS miss file) takes precedence. force_character / force_integer / force_bigint — character vectors variable names whose DuckDB storage type overridden. Unlike conversions , raw string values kept verbatim (numeric conversion, code labeling), geographic codes retain leading zeros --int-range IDs survive. force_character keeps column VARCHAR; force_integer / force_bigint cast INTEGER / BIGINT via ALTER COLUMN table written (INTEGER cast overflows 2^31 errors — use force_bigint). variable may appear one force_* set (including force_numeric); validated build time. LFS sources SURVYEAR / SURVMNTH / REC_NUM integer-forcing mechanism shared LFS registry entry. codes_supplement — named list data.frames injecting code-label rows absent SPSS command files (values present data declared command files, e.g. CHS PPROV territories code). data frame columns val, label_en, label_fr. Setting label_en = NA marks value intentionally missing (produces silent NA factor entry without warning, without introducing spurious factor level). entries verified override ledger (tests/testthat/override_verification.csv). na_values — character vector raw string sentinels become NA. numeric columns exact-matched NA’d numeric conversion; labeled (factor) columns silently blanked. Used undeclared Census income sentinels SAS-style "." missing markers. labels_supplement — named list c(VAR = c(label_en =, label_fr =)) supplying variable labels source metadata leaves blank (e.g. CPSS 1 ships PDF codebook whose weight variable COVID_WT empty Concept: line languages). Applied Stage 3 label_pumf_columns() / pumf_var_labels(), fills NA labels, genuine source labels always win.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: surveys ship several linked files share respondent key meant joined analysis (GSS cycle 16 / Aging Social Support 2002, GSS Time Use cycles, Survey Household Spending 2017, Giving/Volunteering/ Participating cycles). canpumf models several tables inside one DuckDB file — separate databases, joined single connection. registry entry declares modules = list(MAIN = ..., CG4 = ...); module carries layout_mask, file_mask, data_fixups, bootstrap-weight config. One module primary (respondent-level file carries survey weight); config auto-derived entry’s top level single-table code paths keep working unchanged. entry also records module_key — shared key modules join (varies: RECID, PUMFID, MICRO_ID, CASEID, IDNUM). pumf_run_pipeline() loops modules, running Stage 2 Stage 3 per module every table lands one DuckDB file. module parses metadata metadata/<module>/ (primary uses metadata/) joins bootstrap weights, e.g. SHS Interview replicate weights mis-joined onto Diary table. primary module’s tbl returned. User-facing, get_pumf() returns primary module emits one-time message listing sibling modules; pumf_module(tbl, "<module>") opens sibling connection two joinable. dedicated Working multi-module PUMF surveys vignette covers user-facing workflow full.

title: canpumf Pipeline Architecture
text: LFS handled lfs_get_pumf() (delegated directly get_pumf() without going get_pumf_connection()). Instead one DuckDB per version, LFS versions share single <cache_path>/LFS/LFS.duckdb accumulating tables lfs_eng lfs_fra. Key differences standard pipeline: Schema evolution — new LFS version adds variable absent earlier versions, column added via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN. variable changes type (e.g. VARCHAR → ENUM), ALTER COLUMN SET DATA TYPE used. Annual supersedes monthly — annual monthly versions year loaded, annual version supersedes monthly rows year. Version tracking — lfs_versions table shared DuckDB records versions downloaded parsed, refresh = "auto" downloads new versions. Read-fast path — requested version already database, lfs_get_pumf() opens read-connection returns immediately. write lock acquired unless new data actually needs written. get_pumf() return — specific version requested, function applies dplyr::filter() SURVYEAR (SURVMNTH monthly requests) full shared table. Calling get_pumf("LFS") without version returns unfiltered table. label_pumf_columns() LFS — shared schema union loaded versions, variables introduced later years (e.g. GENDER added ~2020) absent older versions’ variables.csv. label_pumf_columns() therefore reads merges metadata every loaded version directory chronological order, -recent label winning conflicts.

title: Generate bootstrap weights for a PUMF dataset — add_bootstrap_weights
text: tbl lazy `dplyr::tbl()` returned [get_pumf()], **** -memory `data.frame` / `tibble`. weight_col Name column holding survey weights (string, e.g. `"PWEIGHT"`). id_col Optional name column uniquely identifies row (DuckDB path ).  `NULL` (default), registry `bsw_join_key` used available; otherwise `pumf_row_id` added main table. strata_cols Optional character vector column names stratify . Resampling performed independently within unique combination stratum values, preserving stratum sample sizes across replicates.  LFS, defaults `c("SURVYEAR", "SURVMNTH")` month resampled separately.  surveys, use registry `bsw_strata` field pass explicitly (e.g. province, age group).  Pass `character(0)` suppress LFS default generate unstratified weights. n_replicates Number bootstrap replicates generate (default `500L`). prefix Column-name prefix replicate columns (default `"CPBSW"`). Columns named `prefix1`, `prefix2`, ... bsw_table Name DuckDB table stores replicate weights (DuckDB path ).  Defaults `NULL`, auto-names `paste0("pumf_bsw_", tolower(weight_col))` separate calls different weight columns overwrite . seed Optional integer seed reproducibility. overwrite `bsw_table` already exists DuckDB file, regenerate overwrite `TRUE`.  `FALSE` (default) existing table reused silently – computation performed.

title: Generate bootstrap weights for a PUMF dataset — add_bootstrap_weights
text: * **DuckDB path:** lazy `dplyr::tbl()` backed persistent DuckDB     VIEW contains original survey columns plus `n_replicates`     bootstrap weight columns, input `filter()` operations re-applied.   * **-memory path:** input `data.frame` / `tibble` bootstrap     weight columns appended `n_replicates` replicates present.     input already carries replicate columns `prefix`,     additional ones generated (existing columns preserved);     already least `n_replicates`, data frame returned unchanged.

title: Generate bootstrap weights for a PUMF dataset — add_bootstrap_weights
text: **-memory `data.frame` `tibble`**, bootstrap weights generated entirely memory augmented data frame returned. Bootstrap weights generated rescaled bootstrap: replicate sample \(n\) rows drawn replacement; bootstrap weight row \(\) replicate \(b\) `original_weight[] * count[,b]`, `count[,b]` number times row \(\) appeared draw \(b\). **Incremental re-runs (DuckDB path):** BSW table already exists call work needed satisfy request:   * **replicates** stored (new rows): additional     replicate columns appended; existing columns kept.   * **New rows** main table (rows weights yet):     bootstrap replicate resamples full population, added rows invalidate     existing weights resampling universe, weights     deleted regenerated.  Unstratified, regenerates every row;     `strata_cols` effect, strata gained rows     regenerated complete strata keep existing weights.   * **Neither:** stored weights reused without recomputation. Pass `overwrite = TRUE` force full fresh regeneration regardless. **Multiple weight columns (hierarchical data):** default `bsw_table` named `weight_col` (e.g. `"pumf_bsw_wstpwgt"`), calling function twice different weight columns (e.g. household weight person weight) produces two independent BSW tables two separate views without conflict. **Connection note (DuckDB path):** calling function fully shuts DuckDB -process instance held `tbl` (write connection requires exclusive access).  input `tbl` lazy tables backed DuckDB file become invalid call.  Use returned tbl instead. **Filtered input tbls (DuckDB path):** bootstrap weights always cover complete physical survey table.  `tbl` dplyr `filter()` operations applied, captured automatically re-applied returned VIEW tbl visible rows match original subset.  operations (`select()`, `mutate()`, etc.) replayed – interfere BSW columns – apply manually returned tbl needed. **ID column (DuckDB path):** stable row identifier needed link main table BSW table.  `id_col` `NULL` (default):   * survey registry `bsw_join_key` used available (e.g.     `"PEFAMID"` SFS 2016-2023) – table modification needed.   * Otherwise `pumf_row_id` column (DuckDB `rowid`) added main     survey table.  `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` O(1); `UPDATE`     fills values O(n).

text: prefer Character vector format tokens order preference; default puts CSV / flat text ahead statistical-package formats. max_surveys Optional integer: crawl first N surveys (useful quick look — full crawl issues hundred requests). surveys Optional character vector catalogue ids restrict . verbose `TRUE`, print progress survey crawled. refresh `FALSE` (default), crawl result cached reused — full crawl expensive (hundreds requests). Within session held memory; *full* crawl (`max_surveys`/`surveys`) also persisted disk `cache_path` survives across sessions. Defaults `getOption("canpumf.cache_path")`; unset durable cache -session cache used. persisted catalogue older `getOption("canpumf.catalogue_max_age_days", 30)` triggers staleness warning suggesting `refresh = TRUE`. live crawl fails (StatCan unreachable) last persisted copy returned warning.

title: Build a labeled DuckDB table for a PUMF version — pumf_build_duckdb
text: version_dir Path returned [pumf_locate_or_download()]. series Survey series acronym, e.g. `"SFS"`. version Version string, e.g. `"2019"`. lang `"eng"` (default) `"fra"`. layout_mask Optional layout mask; used DuckDB table name. file_mask Optional regex select data file.  Overrides registry. refresh `TRUE`, drop rewrite DuckDB table. db_path Optional explicit path DuckDB file.  Defaults `<version_dir>/<series>_<version>.duckdb`.  Multi-module surveys pass one shared path every module table lands file. meta_subdir Optional metadata subdirectory `metadata/` read build.  `NULL` (default) uses `metadata/` (primary module); secondary modules pass module id `metadata/<id>/` read. data_fixups Optional `data_fixups` list overriding registry entry's build.  Used secondary modules, supply complete fixup set (e.g. `force_numeric`), replacing entry's primary fixups. bsw_override Optional list bootstrap-weight config (`bsw_mask`, `bsw_file_mask`, `bsw_join_key`, `bsw_drop_cols`, `bsw_strata`) overriding registry entry's BSW config build. module multi-module survey joins bootstrap weights (none), caller passes module's BSW config; override whose fields `NULL` means "module bootstrap weights".

title: canpumf 0.5.2
text: Experimental list_statcan_pumf_catalogue() crawls live Statistics Canada “Public use microdata” listing returns one row per discovered survey edition (catalogue_id, Title, edition, format, url, product_url) — discovery counterpart curated list_canpumf_collection() picks newly released PUMFs automatically. Editions offered several formats collapse single preferred row (CSV / flat text first), genuinely distinct surveys file-types share reference year kept separate rows (e.g. GSS cycle Giving/Volunteering survey released 2007, census individual/family/household/hierarchical files one year). Surveys distributed Electronic File Transfer report url = "(EFT)". full crawl expensive (hundreds requests), list_statcan_pumf_catalogue() caches result duration R session reuses subsequent calls arguments. Pass refresh = TRUE re-scrape live catalogue replace cached result, e.g. pick newly released survey mid-session. Census PUMF editions decoded cenNN / nhsNN filename prefix (2011 cycle shipped National Household Survey) ind / fam / hous / hier file type canonical "YYYY (individuals)"-style strings matching list_canpumf_collection(). forward-compatible: 2026 census PUMF resolve automatically released. list_statcan_pumf_catalogue() now returns SeriesTitle column (plain-language series name matching acronym) alongside edition-specific Title. umbrella products whose catalogue title series name (e.g. consolidated General Social Survey, census year’s individuals/hierarchical pair) Title synthesised "<series> — <edition>", structural edition descriptor disambiguates colliding reference years ("General Social Survey — Cycle 16 (2002)", "Census Population — 2021 (individuals)"); per-edition products keep Statistics Canada’s title. get_pumf() now resolves download URLs scraped catalogue first series crawler covers (GSS, SHS, SFS, CPSS, CIS, CHS, , CCAHS), newly released edition downloadable without package update. Series crawler deliberately cover — LFS Census (keep dedicated paths) Giving/Volunteering surveys (SGVP, Statistics Canada ships reused zip names umbrella crawl disambiguate) — continue resolve curated list_canpumf_collection(). URL resolution never triggers live crawl; reads cached catalogue. package now ships frozen snapshot full catalogue crawl (inst/extdata/pumf_catalogue.rds). terminal, always-available fallback URL resolution list_statcan_pumf_catalogue(): freshly installed package user cache network still resolves every supported survey’s download URL, change Statistics Canada website silently break get_pumf() releases. shipped snapshot regenerated release.

title: canpumf 0.5.2
text: get_pumf(), get_pumf_connection() pumf_metadata() now fail gracefully Statistics Canada unreachable: download failure longer raises error instead emits informative message returns NULL. list_available_lfs_pumf_versions() likewise returns empty result warning rather erroring, matching existing behaviour list_canpumf_collection() list_statcan_pumf_catalogue(). close_pumf(NULL) now -op, can called unconditionally get_pumf() result may NULL. options(canpumf.cache_path = ) set, package now notes attached first download, explaining data written temporary directory (discarded end session) configure persistent cache. underlying behaviour unchanged — without cache path, data stored tempdir() session.

title: canpumf 0.5.2
text: Surveys whose StatCan ZIP archives carry accented path names stored CP437/Latin-1 without UTF-8 flag (e.g. Survey Household Spending 2017, whose data live Data - Données/ folder) now extract correctly Linux Windows. Previously utils::unzip() either errored “invalid multibyte string” (Windows) silently dropped affected files non-UTF-8 locale (Linux), survey failed import “parseable metadata files found”. Extraction now uses zip::unzip() primary, locale-agnostic extractor every platform (macOS ditto/system-unzip chain retained fallback newer ZIP compression variants), giving uniform cross-platform behaviour. zip new dependency.

title: canpumf 0.5.1
text: Multi-module survey support. Surveys ship several linked files sharing respondent key now modelled several joinable tables one DuckDB file. get_pumf() returns survey’s primary (respondent-level) module emits one-time message listing available sibling modules; pumf_module(tbl, "<module>") opens sibling connection two joinable, announces shared join key. module’s join key recorded registry (module_key) never guessed (varies: RECID, PUMFID, MICRO_ID, CASEID, IDNUM). Converted surveys include GSS cycle 16 / “Aging Social Support” 2002 (MAIN + CG4 + CG6 + CR), GSS Time Use 1998/2010/2015/2022 (Main + Episode), Survey Household Spending 2017 (Interview + Diary, bootstrap weights), Giving/Volunteering/Participating cycles 1997–2010 (MAIN + GS/VD/GIVE/VOLNTR). close_pumf() now also accepts DuckDB connection returned get_pumf_connection(), closing directly, addition lazy dplyr::tbl() returned get_pumf(). New parse_pdf_codebook() metadata parser StatCan bilingual PDF frequency codebooks. recovers variable value labels surveys whose machine-readable companion data file — notably CPSS cycle 1, (unlike CPSS 2–6) ships variables.csv. CPSS 1 now imports full bilingual labels (parity cycles) pdftools installed. Like existing PDF data-dictionary parser, label fallback fires command file codebook CSV found, requires pdftools (Suggests).

title: canpumf 0.5.1
text: New “Working multi-module PUMF surveys” vignette showing load primary module, open sibling modules pumf_module(), join inside DuckDB, use get_pumf_connection() / close_pumf() directly. New “Bootstrap weights” vignette documenting resampling method, weights stored, stratification, estimating uncertainty, incremental re-run behaviour (reuse, adding replicates, regeneration rows added).

title: canpumf 0.5.1
text: get_pumf("LFS") (calls) longer trigger spurious RStudio “Error dbSendQuery(…)” Connections-pane popups. Transient internal DuckDB connections (status checks, write phases, BSW edits) longer registered RStudio Connections pane; final connection returned user registered. add_bootstrap_weights() -memory data.frame/tibble already replicate columns now extends existing set (generating additional replicates) instead regenerating full set producing duplicate column names. matches DuckDB-backed behaviour. add_bootstrap_weights() now handles rows added survey table already bootstrap weights correctly. Previously generated replicates new rows isolation (resampling among new rows), statistically wrong. now deletes regenerates affected weights: every row unstratified, strata gained rows strata_cols effect (complete strata keep existing weights). GSS Time Use 1998 now imports cleanly regardless locale. C locale (R CMD check) list.files() selected Main module’s SAS PROC FORMAT, injected categorical codes onto continuous clock-time, duration, decimal-hour birth-year variables; now declared force_numeric values preserved. addition, merge_metadata() longer warns label conflicts arise solely lossy supplement parsers (SAS labels, PDF dictionary/codebook) — authoritative-source conflicts still warn.

title: canpumf 0.5.0
text: Data now imported DuckDB (breaking change, requiring slight modification code) Adaptable metadata parsing registry Multiple robust strategies parse metadata Better data download import mechanics Extensive test suite prevent regressions catch StatCan re-releases data changed metadata
